Air & Space Forces.title: How the Space Force Is Supporting NASA’s Artemis II Mission
Air & Space Forces.summary: In many ways, the Space Force’s role in NASA’s Artemis II mission, scheduled to lift off from the Eastern Range the evening of April 1, is the same as any other launch it supports. Air & Space Forces.title: A-10 Fleet in Middle East Poised to Double as Jets Cross the Atlantic
Air & Space Forces.summary:
The U.S. military is poised to double its A-10 presence in the Middle East, as the venerable close air support plane plays a key role in combating Iranian boats and Iranian-backed militias in Iraq as part of Operation Epic Fury against Iran.

Air & Space Forces.title: F-35 from Nellis Air Force Base Crashes in Nevada
Air & Space Forces.summary:
A U.S. Air Force F-35A Lightning II fighter from Nellis Air Force Base, Nev., crashed in the sprawling training complex surrounding the base, service officials said March 31. The pilot suffered minor injuries and is being treated after ejecting from the aircraft.

Air & Space Forces.title: NGA Launches New Small Sats to Measure Earth’s Magnetic Field
Air & Space Forces.summary:
Small satellites meant to improve the way the U.S. measures Earth’s magnetic field—an option to expand the military’s position, navigation, and timing enterprise—launched March 30 from Vandenberg Space Force Base, Calif.

Space & Defense.summary: Bright Ascension confirms the successful launch of two VIREON™ Earth observation satellites, developed by AAC Clyde Space. Space & Defense.title: NASA launches Artemis II crew on 10-day Moon flyby test mission
Space & Defense.summary: NASA has launched the crewed Artemis II mission, sending four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft on a planned test flight around the Moon and back. Space & Defense.summary: Southern Launch says it has been selected to support tracking of NASA’s Artemis II Orion mission from South Australia, using passive Doppler tracking infrastructure at the Koonibba Test Range. Space & Defense.title: Unseenlabs says BRO-19 satellite launched on SpaceX Transporter-16 mission
Space & Defense.summary: French space company Unseenlabs says it has successfully launched its BRO-19 satellite, expanding its space-based radio-frequency (RF) detection capabilities for maritime monitoring. Space & Defense.title: QuantX Labs Launches World-First Optical Atomic Clock Technology into Orbit
Space & Defense.summary: QuantX Labs has successfully launched its optical frequency comb into orbit, marking a major milestone in the development of the company’s TEMPO.Space optical atomic clock and bringing the world closer to the first fully operational optical atomic clock in space.
